rationale.root_cause: The response gives two conflicting diagnoses, first claiming an out-of-bounds at program.c:12 before later mentioning the UAF around free/print, so it hedges and does not clearly identify the single FLAW site. Under the rubric, equal hedging with an incorrect cause means no credit.
rationale.local_fix: It suggests both an irrelevant bounds check and the correct "move free after print" change, i.e., it hedges equally between a wrong and a right local fix. Per rules, this ambiguity earns no credit.
rationale.global_fix: It explicitly recommends RAII/smart pointers and, for C, strict ownership conventions to make object lifetime explicit. This matches the required global-fix criterion.
